translated: Jesus taught, saying, "Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God; blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God."

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.

translated: When Yisa went to see with his eyes the word of God, he carried the cross with strength, he found the good path of mercy.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Ŋanne ki sɔɔ din betɔ a “Gotir, a naa nan a waarii see nanyer po ki loon ki bin pak nana.”
translated: He said to the father, "Gotir, I have not seen your face because you left my people to die."

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.

translated: I want to work in the town, the children carry water from the river to the house, and I go to the market and the farm.

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: Linba nee tee n sɔn nba sa te ki Yennu mɔsonn-n set nan barmɔnii, sɔn nba tan sa nyi n gbanant ni, nisaarii bonchiann paak, a Yennu-n nyik chab bi biite na.
translated: When Linba went to Yennu's town for a meeting, and they went to the farm to eat, after the meal, Yennu's words were fulfilled.

translated: When Jonah went to Nineveh to preach to the people, and Bik also spoke to them and provided food when he arrived.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ki li yann waaminna ki jab nba see leŋ na baar ki tan yet Piita a “A set tee bi ni yenɔe, kimaan a pinpakit nee want.”
translated: Peter said to them, "I told you that I am going to see his father."

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “N beerae, fiit, ki jii a liaŋ kii kun a ŋaak ni.”
translated: I spoke, ate, and went to the town and came home.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Ŋanne ki u betib a “Bi daa saa. I mɔŋ-n turib jeet ki bin di.”
translated: Then you said, "He went there. I have already told you the reason."

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: La din nyii li yoo maŋ ki jii saa, ki Yisa piin ki beer u poorpoweiteeb a “See ki n fabir ki saan Jerusalem, ŋaan ti tiŋ ni saakab, nan Yennu jiantu yudamm, nan sennu wannteeb sa dinin fara bonchiann, ki bia tan sa kpima; ŋaan daa ŋanntaa daar ki Yennu sa fiinin kuun ni.”
translated: When they were going to Jerusalem, Jesus spoke to his disciples, saying, "Go into the city to a certain man and tell him, 'The Teacher says, my time is near; I will celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.'"

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Wunba sikint u mɔŋ ki u binbeŋ tee kaman nan bik na binbeŋ na, ŋɔɔe tan sii tee saakɔɔ Yennu naan ni.
translated: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God, whose name is Yennu.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ki Yisa din ki jiin siari; ŋaan ki u poorpoweiteeb betɔ a wun ber poo na, kimaan u mɔk fuut.
translated: When Yisa went to eat, the child carried water on the head to cook food.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Ki Yisa yiin u poorpoweiteeb ki tan yetib a “Ninbaauk mɔkin nan niib na, kimaan daa ŋanntaae na ki bi be n boor nna, ŋaan ki mɔk jeet ki di. N ki loon man kunnib nan komi, ki bi tan sa mann fib nan kon.”
translated: Jesus said to them as they went, "I give you to my sheep, and this price for you, they will come through the way and enter the door. I will take my sheep, and they will take the other sheep that are not mine."
